title: Interaction of spermine with DNA, vitamin C and bovine serum albumin in the unirradiated and gamma irradiated states
author: S. N. Upadhyay
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Institute of Nuclear Medicine & Allied Sciences, Brig. S. K. Mazumdar Road, Delhi-11 0 054, India 

 E-mail: snu@inmas.org 

  Manuscript received 20 July 2004, revised 2 December 2005, accepted 15 December 2005  

 Structural deformability of spermine with radiation dose (maximum 10 Gy) has been proved. Complex formation of spermine with DNA, vitamin C and BSA took place. Calibration and radiation-induced absorption changes in spermine by ninhydrin reagent has been followed quantitatively. Interaction of vitamin C with DNA and their radiation-induced changes have been reported.Interaction of spermine with DNA in the unirradiated and gamma irradiated states in 10 -3  M phosphate buffer and water have been compared. Addition of spermine and vitamin C to DNA makes DNA structure more condensed. Bovine serum albumin also binds with spermine and protects it from radiation-induced degradation.
